concerns.text: FR-006 states 'sufficient number of resamples' without defining the count. SC-002 defines 1000, but FR-006 itself is unverifiable as written. The requirement must explicitly state the number (e.g., '1000 resamples') to be testable.

FR-007 requires sweeping thresholds 'over at least three values' but does not specify the baseline or the delta values in the requirement text. While SC-004 mentions specific values, FR-007 is ambiguous and unverifiable without the specific parameters defined in the requirement itself.

US-1 Acceptance Scenario 3 states 'without dropping >10% of the initial sample'. This is a success criterion for the pipeline, but the FRs (FR-001 to FR-003) do not explicitly mandate this threshold. The requirement to 'not drop >10%' is missing from the Functional Requirements section, making the acceptance scenario unverifiable against the FRs.

US-3 Acceptance Scenario 3 sets a runtime limit of '≤ 6 hours' and memory '≤ 7 GB'. These are performance constraints. While SC-005 covers runtime, there is no corresponding FR mandating the memory limit or the specific runtime bound in the Functional Requirements section. The system requirements are incomplete for verification.
